name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder 7-item Scale
description: A self-administered anxiety screening and severity measure
instructions: The questions below ask about how often and how severe you may experience anxiety symptoms over the past two weeks.

questions.label: Nervous/Anxious
questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by feeling nervous, anxious or on edge?

questions.label: Uncontrollable worry
questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by not being able to stop or control worrying?

questions.label: Excessive worry
questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by worrying too much about different things?

questions.label: Trouble relaxing
questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by trouble relaxing?

questions.label: Restlessness
questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by being so restless that it is hard to sit still?

questions.label: Irritability
questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by becoming easily annoyed or irritated?

questions.label: Feeling afraid
questions.question: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by feeling afraid as if something awful might happen?

questions.label: Functional Impairment
questions.question: If you checked off any problems, how difficult have these problems made it for you to do your work, take care of things at home, or get along with other people?

severityBands.label: Minimal Anxiety
severityBands.description: Treatment for anxiety may not be clinically indicated.

severityBands.label: Mild Anxiety
severityBands.description: Therapist uses clinical judgement about treatment needs based upon knowledge of the client, duration and severity of symptoms.

severityBands.label: Moderate Anxiety
severityBands.description: Treatment goals and interventions target the specific symptoms indicated by client's answers.

severityBands.label: Moderate to Severe Anxiety
severityBands.description: Treatment goals and interventions target the specific symptoms indicated by client's answers. This score often warrants treatment using medication, therapy, or both.

riskRules.message: Patient reports significant functional impairment due to anxiety symptoms

interpretation.clinicalNotes: The GAD-7 is a valid and efficient tool for screening for GAD and assessing its severity in clinical practice and research. A score of 10 or greater on the GAD-7 represents a reasonable cut point for identifying cases of GAD. Cut points of 5, 10, and 15 might be interpreted as representing mild, moderate, and severe levels of anxiety.

actions.minimal: Treatment for anxiety may not be clinically indicated. Monitor as needed.
actions.mild: Use clinical judgement about treatment needs based on duration and severity of symptoms. Watchful waiting; repeat GAD-7 at follow-up.
actions.moderate: Consider treatment; treatment goals and interventions should target the specific symptoms indicated by patient's answers.
actions.severe: Active treatment is warranted. This score often warrants treatment using medication, therapy, or both. Refer to mental health specialist if needed.

interpretation.functionalImpairmentNote: The functional impairment question (Q8) provides context on real-world impact but is not included in the total score.
references: Spitzer RL, Kroenke K, Williams JB, Löwe B. A brief measure for assessing generalized anxiety disorder: the GAD-7. Arch Intern Med. 2006;166(10):1092-1097.
